scenario: Brightline Healthcare uses Camunda 8.8 Tasklist V1 to coordinate 7,200 tasks per day for nurse hand-offs across its wards. A developer wants to add a short descriptive string above each task tile that summarises the case for the assignee without changing the task name or rewriting any of the existing form definitions.
question: Which Camunda 8.8 reserved variable provides this descriptive text on the task tile in Tasklist V1?

options.text: The reserved variable named taskContextDisplayName supplies the descriptive text on the task tile and is unavailable in Tasklist V2 for the same purpose at all.
The reserved variable named taskTitle replaces the task tile name with the value provided and is supported in both Tasklist V1 and Tasklist V2 for descriptive context display.
The reserved variable named contextDescription is appended to the task tile name and is the documented replacement for use in both Tasklist V1 and Tasklist V2 environments today.
The reserved variable named displayName is appended to the task tile name and persists across all forms and follow-up tasks in the same process instance after the first assignment.

a.text: Correct. The docs reserve the variable name taskContextDisplayName exclusively for the context-variable feature and note that context variables are not supported in Tasklist V2 for this purpose.

b.text: Incorrect. The docs do not define a reserved variable named taskTitle for context display; the documented reserved variable name is taskContextDisplayName and it is V1 only.

c.text: Incorrect. The reserved variable name is taskContextDisplayName, not contextDescription, and context variables are explicitly unsupported in Tasklist V2 per the migration note in the docs.

d.text: Incorrect. The docs do not define a displayName variable for context display, nor do they describe automatic persistence of the value across follow-up tasks in the same instance.

explanation: The context variable feature uses the reserved variable name taskContextDisplayName to show a descriptive label on the task tile. The docs warn that this feature is supported in Tasklist V1 only and is not available in Tasklist V2 per the V1 to V2 migration notes.
